name: Fiskars 32" PowerGear2 Bypass Lopper

specs.Blade Type: Bypass (hardened steel, ground to a sharp edge)
specs.Best For: Live green branches, shrubs, small trees up to 2" diameter

whatWorks: The PowerGear2 mechanism multiplies cutting force approximately 3× — a branch that would normally require serious effort cuts with noticeably less grip strength. Many owners with arthritis or reduced hand strength cite this as the deciding feature.
Hardened steel blade holds an edge through multiple seasons without sharpening under typical residential use. The non-stick coating prevents sap and debris from building up on the blade surface.
At ~1.5 lb, the aluminum handles make extended pruning sessions significantly less fatiguing than heavy steel-handled loppers. A full morning of garden pruning is feasible without arm fatigue.

Fiskars has manufactured these loppers essentially unchanged for over a decade — the long track record means replacement parts, clear repair guidance, and a product that's still in production and supported.
worthKnowing: Bypass blade design cuts best on live, green wood — the scissors action leaves a clean cut. For dead, very dry, or hard wood, an anvil lopper (which crushes rather than slices) is more effective.
32" total length limits reach in tight spaces between dense branches or in confined shrubs — longer 36" loppers are available for overhead or hard-to-reach cutting.
2" max diameter is the honest limit — forcing branches larger than 2" risks blade misalignment and handle stress. Stay within spec.
Right-handed grip design — not ambidextrous. Left-handed users will find the grip less natural, though the tool is usable.

commonComplaints.title: Not ideal for dead dry wood…
commonComplaints.body: Not ideal for dead dry wood — tried it on some dead apple tree branches and the bypass action crushed rather than cut. For dead wood, an anvil lopper is better.

commonComplaints.title: 32 inches is short for overhead…
commonComplaints.body: 32 inches is short for overhead branches I can't reach. Had to get a ladder for anything above head height.

commonComplaints.title: 2 inch limit is real —…
commonComplaints.body: 2 inch limit is real — forced it on a thicker branch once and bent the blade slightly. Stay within spec.

commonComplaints.title: Grip design is right-handed —…
commonComplaints.body: Grip design is right-handed — I'm left-handed and it works fine but the handle doesn't feel quite as natural.

verdict: The Fiskars 32" PowerGear2 Bypass Lopper is one of those tools that outperforms its price — at $49 CAD, the PowerGear2 mechanism and hardened bypass blade deliver cutting performance that rivals loppers costing twice as much. The bypass design is best for live wood, the 32" length has real reach limits, and the 2" diameter cap is a hard rule. For routine Canadian garden maintenance — pruning shrubs, fruit trees, and ornamental trees — this lopper is the correct starting point.

realBuyers.quote: Cuts 1-inch branches like they're pencils. PowerGear makes it almost effortless compared to my old loppers.
My hands aren't strong enough for regular loppers. These PowerGear ones I can actually use all morning.
Five seasons in and the blade is still sharp. Canadian winters haven't done anything to the steel.
Spent a whole Saturday pruning my apple trees and hedges. No sore hands the next day — the mechanism does the work.

sources.title: Fiskars PowerGear2 Lopper Review — Gardening Know How

metaDescription: Fiskars 32" PowerGear2 bypass lopper review for Canada: 3× force multiplication, hardened steel blade, non-stick coating. Honest on bypass vs anvil and 2" diameter limit. ~$49 CAD.
introText: The Fiskars 32" PowerGear2 Bypass Lopper uses a gear mechanism that multiplies cutting force 3× — making 2" branches feel like half the effort of standard loppers. Built for live wood pruning in residential Canadian gardens. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B00002N66V) at around $49 CAD.
